meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers Without a Portfolio
content.seo_description: Bridge your portfolio gap with strategic SaaS pricing. Learn to ship high-value projects using Antigravity and Claude Code to win clients immediately.
content.answer_block: When you lack a portfolio, shift your pricing from 'hourly' to 'value-based outcome.' By building a high-impact SaaS pricing calculator for a niche, you demonstrate technical authority and business acumen simultaneously. This transforms your lack of history into a proof-of-concept for the ROI you deliver to future clients.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio page is the freelancer's greatest adversary. You have the technical skills to build, but without a track record, clients see a liability instead of an asset. The tension is palpable: you need the job to get the experience, but you need the experience to get the job. It is a cycle that kills careers before they start.

But what if you stopped waiting for permission and started shipping solutions? By engineering a sophisticated SaaS pricing model, you aren't just showing code—you are showing a client how to make money. This turns your 'no portfolio' problem into a 'business consultant' opportunity. Let's use modern AI tooling to bridge that gap in a single afternoon.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Pricing Logic
steps.description: Before writing code, define the variables that drive SaaS growth: churn reduction, CAC, and LTV. This establishes you as a business partner, not just a coder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model that accounts for tiered subscription logic, usage-based billing, and annual discount calculations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing.ts file based on the generated schema with type-safe interfaces.'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Calculator UI
steps.description: Create a high-fidelity interface that visualizes pricing tiers. Speed matters; use Antigravity to scaffold the component structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a React component using Tailwind CSS that displays three pricing cards with hover states, dynamic price toggling, and a 'Select Plan' call-to-action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Apply a professional, dark-mode aesthetic to the pricing component using Tailwind CSS utility classes.'

steps.title: Implement Dynamic State Management
steps.description: Ensure the calculator reacts instantly to user inputs. This demonstrates your ability to handle complex front-end state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom React hook 'usePricing' that calculates annual savings and updates the UI based on monthly/annual toggle state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the usePricing hook into the pricing cards and add unit tests to verify the math.'

steps.title: Add Lead-Capture Integration
steps.description: Transform the calculator into a lead magnet. Integrate a simple form that sends the 'custom quote' to a mock endpoint.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lead capture form that validates inputs and triggers a console log with the user's selected plan data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Connect the lead form to a mock API endpoint and add success state feedback.'

steps.title: Optimize for Deployment
steps.description: Package the project for production. A live link is worth a thousand lines of code in a PDF portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment-ready configuration for a Vercel-based project, including environment variable setup and build optimization scripts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute build and verify linting passes for production deployment readiness.'

pro_tips: Never display 'Contact Us' as a price—it screams that you don't know your own value. Always provide at least one anchored price point.
Add a 'Recommended' badge to your middle tier; it increases conversion by 30% through the Decoy Effect.
Use Claude Code to generate 'Negative Test Cases' to ensure your pricing math never breaks under extreme usage inputs.
Include a 'What's included' tooltip on every feature to reduce friction for non-technical buyers.
Host your calculator on a sub-domain of your personal site to show potential clients a live, working SaaS asset.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel, share the live link on your LinkedIn profile as a 'SaaS pricing proof-of-concept,' and reach out to three local startups offering to audit their current pricing page.
content.conversion_block: You now have a high-value asset that proves you can build complex, business-critical features. Stop waiting for the perfect client to find you and start showing them exactly how you can increase their revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
